allies.rationale_text: Critical-thinking depends on intellectual-humility because skeptical evaluation only works when the thinker is willing to inspect whether their own frame is failing.
allies.source_quote: Critical thinkers use metacognition to stand outside their mental models and ask with neutrality whether they are working.

allies.rationale_text: First-principles-thinking is strengthened by intellectual-humility because rebuilding from facts requires admitting that the conventional path may not be best.
allies.source_quote: Instead of following conventional means, IH allows one to ask, Are they the best?

allies.rationale_text: Falsifiability needs intellectual-humility because a real disconfirming test is only possible when the thinker is willing to let evidence overturn their favored view.
allies.source_quote: IH is meant to help identify and reconcile disconfirming evidence

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias is the natural antagonist of intellectual-humility because it defends prior belief instead of updating under new evidence.
antagonists.source_quote: Failure to apply IH results in the confirmation bias

antagonists.rationale_text: Authority-bias weakens intellectual-humility when deference replaces firsthand examination of whether the leader, expert, or convention is actually right here.
antagonists.source_quote: criticizing a leadership proposal can be viewed as undermining the vision

structured_tensions.tension_text: Intellectual-humility conflicts with confidence-calibration when excessive humility systematically underweights well-supported positions that deserve higher confidence.
structured_tensions.source_quote: intellectual-humility conflicts with confidence-calibration (confidence calibration) when excessive humility systematically underweights well-supported positions that deserve higher confidence.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Intellectual-humility conflicts with authority-bias when humility about one's own views defaults to uncritical acceptance of the authority's position.
structured_tensions.source_quote: intellectual-humility conflicts with authority-bias (authority bias) when humility about one's own views defaults to uncritical acceptance of the authority's position.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on critical, first-principles, and falsifiability allies plus direct conflict with defended certainty, so the file preserves that compact surface.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor relation widening beyond the clearly named updating, anti-overconfidence, and anti-deference neighborhood.
open_questions: Feynman-technique is source-real ally pressure too, but the retained set favors already-curated models with cleaner explicit updating language in the source.
